summarization.a: The figure displays XANES absorption spectra of various manganese oxides at different lithium concentrations.
summarization.b: The figure displays EXAFS  spectra of various manganese oxides at different lithium concentrations.
summarization.c: The figure shows Mn L-edge XANES spectra measured in 45° incident angle

data_extraction.a:
| Sample          | Energy Range (eV) | Key Features                                  |
|-----------------|--------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| MnO₂           | 6540–6580         | Strong edge at ~6555 eV; post-edge oscillations |
| 10cLi-MnO₂     | 6540–6580         | Slight edge shift; reduced intensity          |
| 50cLi-MnO₂     | 6540–6580         | Further edge shift; more pronounced changes   |
| MnO₂ ref.      | 6540–6580         | Baseline for Mn⁴⁺                             |
| LiMn₂O₄ ref.   | 6540–6580         | Spinel reference                              |

c.question: What is the difference between K-edge and L-edge?
c.answer:
1. The L-edge XANES measures the absorption by the electric dipole-allowed transition from the
2p orbital to the unfilled 3d orbital of a transition metal resulting in an intense and feature-rich LII,III-edge XANES spectra.
2. The K-edge measures dipole-forbidden transition from the 1s orbital to the 3d orbital and hence are of lower-intensity.

a.question: Does sample  β-MnO2−50cLi−MnO2 have comparable to the β-MnO2 reference?
What is the reference used for the crystal structure of LiMn2O4?

b.question: What happens when lithium is inserted?
b.answer: Immediate reduction of manganese occurs as soon as lithium is inserted, as the absorption edge shifts to lower energy in the Mn K-edge XANES.
